data.body:
Goal:
Replace the per-agent interactive watchdog constants with one shared interactive-session timeout owned by the common runtime/settings layer. The timeout must default to 10 minutes, be editable in the Settings UI, and apply uniformly to every interactive agent session without requiring per-harness timeout code. Today the four interactive implementations that must converge on this shared path are Codex (`jsonrpc-stdio`), Copilot (`stdin-stream-json`), OpenCode (`http-server`), and Gemini (`acp-stdio`). Future interactive harnesses should inherit the timeout automatically by using the shared interactive session abstraction, with no harness-specific timeout wiring.

Verification:
1. Open Settings -> Defaults and confirm an interactive session timeout control is present, defaults to 10 minutes for a fresh settings state, and persists edits after save/reload.
2. Run `bun run test` and confirm the interactive-session/runtime/settings test suite passes with coverage for the shared timeout source and settings round-trip.
3. Inspect the interactive session capability/runtime path and confirm the timeout is sourced once from shared settings/runtime code, not hardcoded separately in Codex, Copilot, OpenCode, or Gemini capability presets.

Constraints:
- The timeout must be modeled in shared settings/schema code, not as per-agent literals in `src/lib/agent-session-capabilities.ts`.
- Interactive harness implementations must not own timeout policy; they should only participate in the shared session abstraction that enforces it.
- The design must make new interactive harnesses inherit the timeout automatically without additional timeout-specific implementation work.
- Keep the Settings UI wording explicit that this controls the inactivity timeout for interactive agent sessions.
data.title: Globalize interactive agent timeout
